lines: The world's hardest test for firefighting robots just ran in Alaska.
No crew, no pilot. It finds a fire and puts it out on its own.
The X Prize Wildfire finals. Eleven million dollars, four years long.
Nearly three hundred teams. Three reached the final. All came to Nenana.
The University of Alaska ran the airspace over it.
A sensor network smells the smoke in minutes and wakes a drone.
It flies out, finds the flame, drops it. No one touches a controller.
Dryad, one of the finalists, says it worked.
But this was one test fire on an airstrip, not a megafire in roadless country.
And no winner is named until September.
For once, the AI did not come to take from Alaska. It came to protect it.
Alaska did not build these machines. It built the range they had to survive.
scene_map: S1 lines 0-1, S2 2-3, S3 4-5, S4 6-7, S5 8-9, S6 10-12 (matches scripts/build_scenes.py SCENE_START_LINE [0,2,4,6,8,10])

phonetic_and_display_notes.X Prize: spoken 'Ex Prize'; on screen render as 'XPRIZE'
phonetic_and_display_notes.ACUASI: NOT spoken in VO; appears only on screen as 'UAF ACUASI'
phonetic_and_display_notes.numbers: spoken as words; ON SCREEN render as numerals: $11,000,000 (or $11M), 4 years, ~300 teams, 3 finalists, September 2026
phonetic_and_display_notes.up to 100 liters: not in VO; on-screen annotation only, labeled 'per Dryad'

attribution_guardrails: Line 8 must read as Dryad's OWN claim (on screen: 'Dryad SAYS'); lines 9-10 carry the honest turn (controlled test, judging pending Sept 2026). ACUASI = 'managed the airspace'. Silvanet/Silvaguard figures labeled 'per Dryad'. On-screen numbers illustrative.
target_seconds: 50 to 67; if the synth runs long, TRIM (do not time-stretch).
caption_fixups_note: On-screen captions are force-aligned from Whisper's transcript of the PHONETICALLY-respelled audio, so proper-noun respellings ('Ex Prize', 'DRY-ad', 'nuh-NAN-uh'->'Nana') leak onto screen as typos. build_scenes.py applies these display-spelling fixups to every caption cue (case-insensitive, word-boundary) so the REAL spelling always shows. Add a run's phonetic->display pairs here.
